matchup: New York Yankees @ Kansas City Royals

pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: The Cole 5+ market was a threshold mispricing. The play was not built as an aggressive ceiling ladder because of possible post-ramp leash concerns, but plus money on 5+ created strong price-to-probability value.

pitch_notes.four_seam_fastball: 32 pitches, 4 K, 96.4 mph avg, 31% CSW; fastball established velocity floor and strike pressure.
pitch_notes.slider: 19 pitches, 3 K, 60% whiffs per swing, 47% CSW; main putaway pitch.
pitch_notes.changeup: 14 pitches, 2 K, 44% whiffs per swing, 43% CSW; excellent secondary support.
pitch_notes.knuckle_curve: 12 pitches, 1 K, 50% CSW; added third/fourth pitch K depth.

what_worked: The model correctly identified plus money on Cole 5+ as a price-to-threshold error.
Leash concern was overcautious; Cole delivered 6.2 IP on only 79 pitches.
Zero walks and 75% strike rate created elite runway stability.
Cole exceeded the threshold by five strikeouts, finishing with 10 Ks.
Multiple pitches generated K support, preventing single-pitch dependency.
what_to_modify: When Cole-type veterans show normal velo and strike efficiency early, upgrade post-ramp leash assumptions faster in LIVEFLOW.
Do not over-penalize post-ramp status if pitch count efficiency is elite through first two innings.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: KEEP PRICE_TO_THRESHOLD_K_EDGE. Add VETERAN_RAMP_CLEARANCE override when early velocity, strike rate, and pitch economy confirm normal workload viability.

notes: This was the best price-to-threshold result on the card. Cole's 5+ at +100 closed as a major value because the actual outing produced 10 Ks, 39% CSW, 35% whiff rate on swings, zero walks, and only 79 pitches through 6.2 IP. The model correctly captured the baseline threshold edge while appropriately avoiding unnecessary ladder exposure.
